channel.title: Homepage on Wang's Harmony Acupuncture LLC
channel.description: Recent content in Homepage on Wang's Harmony Acupuncture LLC

item.title: Acupuncture Consultation and Personalized Care
item.description: Start with a brief virtual consultation, then mobile acupuncture visits with clear follow-up planning and fair pricing.

item.title: What Is Acupuncture?
item.description:
Acupuncture is one of the main treatments in Traditional Chinese Medicine. It has been practiced for centuries, with early written records dating back to around 100 BCE.

In Chinese medicine, health is connected to balance in yin and yang, qi (chee), blood, and the functional harmony of organs and meridians. When that balance is disrupted, symptoms can develop.

Acupuncture uses very thin needles placed at specific points on the body to help restore balance. The goal is to support the body’s natural healing response and ease symptoms over time.

item.title: Dr. Dan Wang
item.description:
Originally from Northeast China, Dan brings a deep cultural and clinical background in Traditional Chinese Medicine. She earned her degree from the Liaoning University of Traditional Chinese Medicine in the 1990s and has practiced in the western suburbs of Chicago for the past four years.

Dan is deeply interested in Chinese medical philosophy and believes healing is strongest when care is calm, personalized, and grounded in balance. She works closely with each patient to understand their symptoms, explain the treatment plan clearly, and support steady progress over time.

item.title: Acute and Chronic Pain Management
item.description: Acupuncture care for musculoskeletal pain, headaches, nerve-related symptoms, and injury recovery.

item.title: Does Acupuncture Hurt?
item.description:
Acupuncture needles are hair-thin filiform needles. They are very different from the hollow needles used for injections or blood draws, and insertion is painless or only mildly noticeable for most people.

Points on the hands, feet, and face can sometimes feel more sensitive, but that usually lasts only a moment.

During treatment, some people feel soreness, numbness, heaviness, warmth, coolness, or brief itching. These sensations are usually temporary.

After treatment, many people feel calm and deeply relaxed. That sense of settling is one of the ways people first notice acupuncture working.

item.title: How Much Does Mobile Acupuncture Cost?
item.description:
Our mobile service currently has a base commute rate of $29.99 per commute.

Before booking further appointments, patients complete a brief virtual consultation for $20.

We keep acupuncture rates fair and offer package options and senior discounts.

For package and senior discount information, call 630-408-7875.

item.title: Women's Health and Fertility Support
item.description: Holistic acupuncture care for cycle health, fertility, hormonal transitions, and pelvic wellness.

item.title: Digestive and Metabolic Health
item.description: Support for abdominal discomfort, IBS, and metabolic health concerns including cholesterol, weight, and blood sugar balance.

item.title: How Many Sessions Will I Need?
item.description:
The number of sessions depends on your condition and how long you have had it.

In general, long-standing issues take longer to treat than newer concerns.

Acupuncture supports the body’s healing response, so it can take time to see clear and lasting results.

A typical recommendation is about twice per week for severe pain, or weekly treatment for four to six weeks for many other concerns before deciding how much it is helping.

item.title: Respiratory, Immune, and Skin Support
item.description: Acupuncture for allergy, asthma, sinus, cough, skin, and facial nerve concerns.

item.title: What Can Acupuncture Treat?
item.description:
Some of the most common reasons patients come to us include pain, anxiety, sinus congestion, insomnia, digestion concerns, fertility support, IVF support, postpartum recovery, and menopause care.

Acupuncture can help reduce pain after injury, but it is wise to see a medical doctor first to rule out fractures or structural damage before starting treatment.

item.title: Stress, Sleep, and Preventive Wellness
item.description: Whole-person acupuncture care for anxiety, fatigue, insomnia, addiction support, and healthy aging.

item.title: What Are the Risks of Acupuncture?
item.description:
Licensed acupuncturists receive thorough training in needling technique and safety, so the risk of serious side effects is very low.

The most common side effects are minor bleeding, mild bruising at the needle site, or occasional dizziness after treatment.

Special caution is needed for people taking blood thinner medication.

Electro-acupuncture may not be suitable for people with pacemakers. Acupuncture without electrical stimulation is still available for those patients.

item.title: What Is Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM)?
item.description:
Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) has a history of thousands of years and remains an important part of traditional Chinese culture.

It looks at how the body functions, how patterns of imbalance develop, and how those patterns can be addressed through diagnosis, prevention, and treatment.

Its framework is shaped by ideas such as Yin-Yang, the Five Elements, organ systems, meridians, and syndrome differentiation. In practice, that means treatment is based on the whole picture rather than one symptom by itself.

item.title: About
item.description:
This page has moved.

Please visit About for clinic and practitioner information.

item.title: Contact
item.description:
We welcome new and returning patients. Call or email to book your first appointment, ask about treatment plans, or find out whether acupuncture is a good fit for your goals.

Appointment options

  • Brief virtual consultation before further booking: $20
  • Mobile service base commute rate: $29.99 per commute
  • We provide package options and senior discounts

For package and senior discount information, call 630-408-7875.
